title: GitHub Review by greptile-apps[bot]
summary: The reviewer wants two changes: (1) `EnsureService` should stop mutating the caller's `svc` argument — use a deep copy of the spec instead of modifying `svc` fields directly, keeping it consistent with other `Ensure*` functions. (2) `RunJob` should return a sentinel error (`ErrJobFailed`) for permanent job failures instead of an opaque error string, so callers can use `errors.Is` to distinguish permanent failures from transient ones and avoid pointless requeue loops.

code_comments.body:
**`EnsureService` mutates the caller's `svc` argument**

The function modifies the fields of the passed-in `svc` object directly (lines 75–88) before copying it into `existing.Spec`. After the call returns, the caller's `svc` will have its `ClusterIP`, `ClusterIPs`, and any `NodePort` fields overwritten with the API-server-assigned values. This is an unexpected side-effect that is inconsistent with every other `Ensure*` function in this PR — for example, `EnsureDeployment` does `existing.Spec = deploy.Spec` and never writes back into `deploy`.

A concrete failure scenario: if a reconciler calls `EnsureService` and then inspects `desiredSvc.Spec.ClusterIP` expecting the empty string it set (to detect "not yet assigned"), it will instead see the live ClusterIP. Similarly, comparing the desired spec against a stored baseline will produce spurious diffs.

The fix is to work entirely on the `existing` object, avoiding any mutation of `svc`:

```go
// Preserve the ClusterIP assigned by the API server.
existing.Spec = svc.Spec
existing.Spec.ClusterIP = svc.Spec.ClusterIP   // desired value preserved; will be
existing.Spec.ClusterIPs = svc.Spec.ClusterIPs  // overridden below with server-assigned ones
existing.Spec.ClusterIP = /* prior */ existing.Spec.ClusterIP
```

A cleaner approach — copy the desired spec into a local variable so `svc` is never touched:

```go
newSpec := *svc.Spec.DeepCopy()
newSpec.ClusterIP = existing.Spec.ClusterIP
newSpec.ClusterIPs = existing.Spec.ClusterIPs
for i := range newSpec.Ports {
    if newSpec.Ports[i].NodePort != 0 {
        continue
    }
    for _, ep := range existing.Spec.Ports {
        if newSpec.Ports[i].Port == ep.Port && (newSpec.Ports[i].Protocol == "" || newSpec.Ports[i].Protocol == ep.Protocol) {
            newSpec.Ports[i].NodePort = ep.NodePort
            break
        }
    }
}
existing.Spec = newSpec
if err := c.Update(ctx, existing); err != nil { ...
```

This keeps `svc` immutable from the caller's perspective, consistent with the rest of the package.

Similarly, comparing the desired spec against a stored baseline will produce spurious diffs.

code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: EnsureService now uses `newSpec := *svc.Spec.DeepCopy()` to build the merged spec (preserving ClusterIP, ClusterIPs, and NodePorts from the existing Service) without mutating the caller's svc argument, exactly matching the reviewer's 'cleaner approach' suggestion. Three new tests were added: TestEnsureService_doesNotMutateCallerObject (verifies caller's object is untouched), TestEnsureService_updatesPreservingNodePorts (verifies NodePort preservation), and TestEnsureService_preservesNodePortWhenProtocolOmitted (verifies fallback matching when Protocol is omitted).

code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Exported `var ErrJobFailed = errors.New("job has permanently failed")` sentinel error and changed the RunJob failure return from `fmt.Errorf("job %s/%s has permanently failed", ...)` to `fmt.Errorf("%w: %s/%s", ErrJobFailed, ...)`, exactly as the reviewer suggested. The existing TestRunJob_existingFailed test was updated to assert `errors.Is(err, ErrJobFailed)` instead of string-matching, and to verify the error message includes the namespace/name.

resolution_summary: Both review comments were fully addressed in commit 733e7d2. EnsureService was refactored to use DeepCopy so the caller's svc argument is never mutated, and RunJob now returns a sentinel ErrJobFailed error wrapping via %w for errors.Is compatibility. Additionally, two changes were made beyond the review scope: (1) IsDeploymentReady gained an ObservedGeneration < Generation guard to prevent false readiness on stale status, and (2) EnsureDeployment now re-fetches the Deployment after Update to get the server-assigned Generation — both proactive correctness improvements consistent with the reviewer's theme of avoiding subtle state bugs.
